keyword.cluster: multi-agent frameworks comparison LangGraph CrewAI OpenAI SDK 2026
keyword.commercial_intent: very high — framework selection drives purchasing decisions
keyword.trend_momentum: explosive — multi-agent systems declared '2026's defining AI paradigm' across analyst consensus. Gartner: multi-agent generative systems (MAGS) require specialized software engineering with event-driven architecture. LangGraph at 39.2M PyPI downloads/month, CrewAI at 46.3K GitHub stars, OpenAI Agents SDK now supports 100+ models via LiteLLM. AAAI 2026 Bridge Program dedicated to LLM-based multi-agent collaboration. Microsoft merged AutoGen + Semantic Kernel into unified Microsoft Agent Framework (Oct 2025). AWS published enterprise swarm intelligence architecture. Google ADK launched 2026. 8+ major frameworks now compete for production workloads.

selection_rationale.why_this_keyword: Multi-Agent AI Frameworks comparison is the practical capstone to AgentForge's enterprise AI lifecycle series. Runs #1–4 covered what to build (orchestration, governance, evaluation, deployment) — Run #5 covers which tools to do it with. This is the article every engineering lead searches for after absorbing the conceptual pieces. Framework selection drives real purchasing decisions: a CTO evaluating LangGraph vs CrewAI vs OpenAI SDK is 2–4 weeks from committing budget.
timeliness_factors: 2026 declared 'Year of Multi-Agent Systems' by multiple analyst reports — AI Agents Directory, arXiv/AAAI, and numerous framework vendors
LangGraph hitting 39.2M monthly PyPI downloads — production adoption benchmark reached
OpenAI Agents SDK now model-agnostic via LiteLLM (100+ models) — vendor lock-in concern dissolving
Google ADK launched as new entrant — reshuffling the competitive landscape
Microsoft merged AutoGen + Semantic Kernel into unified Microsoft Agent Framework — Azure-native play
AAAI 2026 Bridge Program on LLM-Based Multi-Agent Collaboration — academic legitimacy achieved
AWS published Enterprise Swarm Intelligence architecture guide — cloud vendor endorsement

Perfect practical capstone to series: after orchestration (how to design), governance (how to control), evaluation (how to measure), deployment (how to scale) — now: which framework to actually build with
selection_rationale.domain_alignment: Perfect — AgentForge's core domain is AI agents and enterprise automation. A framework comparison piece has the highest commercial intent in the series because it directly influences tool procurement. This is the article where readers move from education to action.
selection_rationale.differentiation_from_prior_runs: Run #1 (May 23): AI Agent Orchestration — architectural patterns. Run #2 (May 25): Agentic AI Governance — control frameworks. Run #3 (May 26): AI Agent Evaluation — testing/metrics. Run #4 (May 27): Enterprise AI Agent Deployment — scaling to production. Run #5 (today): Multi-Agent AI Frameworks — hands-on tool comparison. Unique angle: practical procurement guide, not conceptual.
